title: requiredRoles [scout builder auditor] declared independently in 3 integrity packages with LIVE semantic drift: cyclehealth only counts kind==agent_subprocess ledger entries, so Go-native cycles (which emit kind==phase) fail its completeness check while passing ledgerverify
summary: Three sites: ledgerverify/verify.go:53 (var, plus canonicalRole mapping build→builder/audit→auditor, accepts agent_subprocess AND phase kinds), cyclehealth/cyclehealth.go:203 (var, agent_subprocess ONLY — diverged), redteamcheck/redteamcheck.go:108 (inline literal, oldest). The same predicate — 'did all required roles run?' — evaluates differently per package TODAY: a Go-orchestrator cycle satisfies ledgerverify but fails cyclehealth.checkLedgerCompleteness. Adding a mandatory role (e.g. tdd-engineer) to one site silently isn't enforced by the others.
root_cause: Role vocabulary + dual-kind ledger semantics were copy-declared per consumer instead of exported from one source, violating single-source-with-projection.
fix: Export RequiredRoles + the canonicalRole mapping + the accepted-kinds predicate from ONE home (ledgerverify is the natural owner, or a slim internal/cyclecontracts if import direction demands). cyclehealth and redteamcheck import it; cyclehealth adopts the dual-kind acceptance so Go-native cycles evaluate identically everywhere. Pin the set against phasecontract.Contracts() (phases with AgentName != "") so registry evolution can't silently diverge from the role set.
acceptance: RED: TestCycleHealth_GoNativePhaseKindEntriesSatisfyCompleteness — fixture ledger with kind==phase entries for scout/builder/auditor passes cyclehealth (fails today)
RED: cross-package pin — RequiredRoles == roles derived from phasecontract.Contracts(); single declaration site (grep proves the other two literals deleted)
Twin: legacy agent_subprocess-only ledger still passes both packages
connects_to: artifact-name-ssot-retro-backfill (same SSOT family, same phasecontract anchor)
